$comment: Neutral shipped <sys/types.h> (POSIX) — the base system typedefs, as language-NEUTRAL aliases injected by the semantic phase (see README + stdint.json). FIRST shipped descriptor in a SUBDIRECTORY: `#include <sys/types.h>` maps to `sys/types.json` (resolveSystemDescriptor preserves the subdir), DISTINCT from a top-level `<time.h>` -> `time.json` (so the later `<sys/time.h>` -> `sys/time.json` cannot collide). Widths are the LP64 model shared by the runnable unix targets (linux x86-64 + arm64): off_t/ssize_t/blksize_t/blkcnt_t/suseconds_t = the C `long` (i64); dev_t/ino_t/nlink_t = unsigned long (u64); mode_t/uid_t/gid_t/id_t/useconds_t = unsigned int (u32); pid_t/key_t = int (i32). These are the EXACT glibc x86-64 + arm64 (LP64, identical) widths the `struct stat`/`struct flock`/`struct timeval` layouts (later cycles) depend on, and they are the RUN-GREEN target (linux). macOS differs for several (mode_t = u16, dev_t = i32, nlink_t = u16, blksize_t = i32; off_t/ssize_t/uid_t/gid_t/pid_t/ino_t agree) — a descriptor baking a single platform's widths, deferred D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH (the same class as the stdint/stddef ILP32 deferral). It is INERT for the self-contained `shipped_sys_types` corpus (scalar `mode_t = 0` etc. — the width never affects the result); it FIRST bites at the FFI boundary (the macOS `struct stat` field layout, a later cycle), where a per-target width resolution closes it. time_t/clock_t are owned by <time.h> (their canonical C home); sys/types.h legally re-provides them but the first-wins injection dedup makes the owner moot, so they are omitted here to keep one canonical home. No `library` (typedefs have no link surface); no `symbols`/`constants`/`macros`.
